tables.title: Preeclampsia vs. Eclampsia
rows: New-onset hypertension (typically >20 weeks) + proteinuria, or hypertension + other organ dysfunction
Preeclampsia + new seizures or coma not attributable to another cause
Severe headache, visual disturbance, RUQ/epigastric pain, sudden edema
Same warning signs, plus active or recent seizure/coma
Left lateral positioning, minimize stimulation, monitor BP, transport
Seizure precautions, left lateral positioning once feasible, minimize stimulation, rapid transport
Hospital-level management, often including magnesium sulfate for severe disease
